summary: Joel Spolsky announces the redesign of Fog Creek Software's website, featuring a new logo by Sam Sherwood and page design by Dave Shea, known for the CSS Zen Garden and Mozilla's homepage. The site was built with additional work by Fog Creek employee Dmitri Kalmar. Joel notes the site is approximately 99% web standards-compliant, with only a few legacy FONT tags remaining from old content.
key_points: Fog Creek's website was redesigned with a new logo and modern page design
Dave Shea, creator of CSS Zen Garden, handled the web design
The site achieved roughly 99% web standards compliance, reflecting the era's push toward CSS-based layouts
money_quotes: It's about 99% standards-compliant (with the exception of a couple of stray FONT tags left over from old content that hasn't been updated… oh the horror!).
key_insight: This is a brief announcement post about Fog Creek's website redesign, notable mainly for its reflection of the early-2000s web standards movement and Joel's self-deprecating humor about lingering legacy markup.
